entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop species being studied for aflatoxin contamination and resistance.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in peanuts occurring at pre-harvest, post-harvest, and storage stages.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to resist aflatoxin contamination, a priority trait for breeding.

entities.name: INTEGRATED AFLATOXIN MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A comprehensive management approach where aflatoxin resistance is an important component.

entities.name: FIELD SCREENING PROTOCOLS
entities.description: Procedures used to evaluate peanut genotypes for resistance in the field.

entities.name: SAMPLING STRATEGY
entities.description: The approach used to collect samples from field plots for aflatoxin analysis.

entities.name: PLOT SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the experimental plot, identified as key to reducing variability.

entities.name: MAGIC POPULATION
entities.description: Multi-parent Advanced Generation Inter-Cross population lines used as genotypes in the study.

entities.name: J11
entities.description: A known resistant peanut variety used as a standard check.

entities.name: JL 24
entities.description: A known susceptible peanut variety used as a standard check.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The institution and location where the field experiment was conducted.

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to arrange peanut genotypes in the field.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: The toxigenic fungus used to inoculate peanut plots to induce aflatoxin contamination.

entities.name: INDIRECT COMPETITIVE ELISA
entities.description: The laboratory method used for the quantification of aflatoxin in peanut pods.

entities.name: STANDARD ERROR
entities.description: A statistical measure of variability that is minimized by optimizing plot size.
